lines: Your AI coding assistant forgets everything the moment you close the chat. What if it did not?
Every new session it starts cold. No memory of your codebase, no memory of yesterday's decision.
This is Ptah, the AI coding orchestra. A persistent, multi-agent coding agent that lives on your desktop.
It remembers. It learns. It ships. Persistent memory, compounding skills, and up to nine agents working in parallel.
Here is the problem Ptah solves. Most AI tools treat your agent like a new hire that nobody onboarded.
Ptah onboards its agents the way you would onboard an engineer. It studies the codebase before the first message.
And this is Ptah running against real projects. No slideware. Real memory recall and scheduled runs, captured as they happened.
Pillar one. It remembers your codebase, every single session.
Ptah indexes your project before the first message, and keeps what it learns long after the last one.
Persistent memory, code indexing, and hybrid search. Ask where you validate auth tokens in plain English, and get cited results.
Pillar two. It gets better every session. Then it multiplies itself.
Repeat a workflow, and Ptah turns it into a reusable skill, judged and promoted automatically. No manual authoring required.
Then it scales. A main agent fans work out to parallel sub-agents, each with its own model and context window.
Run up to nine concurrent agents in one drag and resize grid. They keep working while you focus on a single tile.
Pillar three. It works while you sleep, and it answers where you already are.
Schedule agents like cron jobs. Nightly code reviews, dependency scans, standup summaries. No server to babysit.
Trigger and approve agent work from Telegram, Discord, or Slack, including with your voice.
And nothing runs unattended that you have not authorized. You approve every tool call before it executes.
No lock-in. Bring any model, and switch providers mid-session without losing your context.
So how is this different? Autocomplete ends at the cursor. Ptah does not.
Cursor and Copilot finish your sentence. Ptah remembers yesterday, works while you are away, and answers from your phone.
Prefer your editor or a terminal? Ptah also ships as a VS Code extension and a headless command line tool.
It remembers. It learns. It ships. Start your one hundred day free trial. No credit card required.
Download Ptah today at Ptah dot live, and give your codebase an AI employee that finally remembers.
